{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Liberia women's national football team}}
{{Use mdy dates|date=March 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Liberia
| Nickname           = The Lone Star
| Badge              = Liberia Football Association logo.svg
| Badge_size         = 200px
| Association        = [[Liberia Football Association]] (LFA)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Thomas Kojo]]
| Captain            = [[Oscar Dorley]]
| Most caps          = [[Joe Nagbe]] (77)
| Top scorer         = [[George Weah]] (18)
| Home Stadium       = [[Samuel Kanyon Doe Sports Complex|SKD Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = LBR
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|LBR}}
| FIFA max           = 66
| FIFA max date      = July 2001
| FIFA min           = 164
| FIFA min date      = October–November 2010
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Liberia}}
| Elo max            = 67
| Elo max date       = March 2001
| Elo min            = 151
| Elo min date       = February 2009
| First game         = {{flagicon|FRA|1830}} [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]] 0–2 {{fb-rt|LBR}} <br> ([[Abidjan]], Ivory Coast; unknown date 1954)
| Largest win        = {{fb|Liberia}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|DJI}} <br> ([[Monrovia]], [[Liberia]]; 29 March 2016)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|GHA}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|LBR}} <br> ([[Accra]], [[Ghana]]; 6 April 1975)
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 2
| Regional cup first = [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
| Regional cup best  = Group stage ([[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]] and [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]])
| pattern_b1         =_lbr25h
| pattern_ra1        =_lbr25h
| pattern_sh1        =_whitebottom
| pattern_so1        =
| leftarm1           = ff0000
| body1              =ff0000
| rightarm1          = ff0000
| shorts1            = ff0000
| socks1             = ff0000
| pattern_la2=_lbr25a
| pattern_b2=_lbr25a
| pattern_ra2=_lbr25a
| pattern_sh2=_whitebottom
| leftarm2=0000ff
| body2=0000ff
| rightarm2=0000ff
| shorts2=0000ff
| socks2=0000FF
|pattern_la1=_lbr25h
| American           = yes|socks3=ffffff|shorts3=ffffff|rightarm3=ffffff|body3=ffffff|leftarm3=ffffff|pattern_sh3=_bluebottom|pattern_ra3=_lbr25t|pattern_b3=_lbr25t|pattern_la3=_lbr25t}}

The '''Liberia national football team''', nicknamed the ''Lone Stars'', represents [[Liberia]] in men's international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Liberia Football Association]]. Although the nation produced the 1995 [[FIFA World Player of the Year]], [[George Weah]], its football team has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]] and has qualified for the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] just twice—in [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]] and [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]. It is a member of both [[FIFA]] and the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).

== History ==

===African Cup of Nations===
In 1965 Liberia played for the first time in the [[1965 African Cup of Nations qualification|1965 qualification]] qualifying campaign, losing its first match 0–1 against [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]]. They also won 3–2 against [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] but lost both returning fixtures and were eliminated in the first round.
In 1967 Liberia played in the [[1968 African Cup of Nations qualification|1968 qualification]] qualifying campaign, drawing its first match 2–2 against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]]. They also drew against [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]] but lost both returning fixtures and were eliminated again in the first round. Liberia returned to qualifying in 1976 but lost in the preliminary round to [[Togo national football team|Togo]], falling in both fixtures. After another absence, this time of six years, Liberia again joined AFCON qualifying in 1982 in the preliminary rounds but failed to progress after two draws against [[Gambia national football team|Gambia]], falling short as a result of the [[away goals rule]].

Liberia withdrew from qualifying for the [[1984 African Cup of Nations]], but the following tournament they managed to secure their first win during qualifying, a 3–1 first-leg victory over [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]]. They failed to capitalize on this advantage, however, and lost 3–0 in the second leg. They then faced [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] and [[Mali national football team|Mali]] in the [[1988 African Cup of Nations qualification|1988 qualification]] and [[1990 African Cup of Nations qualification|1990 qualification]] campaigns respectively, but again failed to progress. In 1992, Liberia withdrew from qualifying before playing a match due to the ongoing [[First Liberian Civil War]], and in 1994, Liberia was put into a group with two participants that withdrew during qualifying ([[Tanzania national football team|Tanzania]] and [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]]) however they failed to take advantage of this and finished with zero points, having lost to [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] twice.

In the [[1996 African Cup of Nations qualification]], led by [[Ballon d’Or]] winner and captain [[George Weah]], Liberia managed to register three wins—against [[Togo national football team|Togo]], [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] and [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]]—and four draws, which saw them finish the group in second place and qualify for their first [[African Cup of Nations]] tournament. Following the withdrawal of [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]], Liberia was placed in a group with [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]] and [[Zaire national football team|Zaire]]. They opened the tournament with a 2–1 victory over Gabon with goals from [[Kelvin Sebwe]] and [[Mass Sarr Jr.]] but lost 2–0 to Zaire. This meant that Liberia finished bottom of the group on goal difference and failed to progress to the knock-out stages.

Liberia missed out on returning to the African Cup of Nations in 1998 as they finished one point off of qualification. In 2000, they defeated [[Niger national football team|Niger]] in the preliminary rounds, but again failed to advance to the main tournament, this time finishing behind [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]] on goal difference. 

In [[2002 African Cup of Nations qualification]], Liberia beat [[Cape Verde national football team|Cape Verde]] in the preliminary rounds, ultimately finishing top of their group to qualify for the tournament for the second time in their history. In the [[2002 African Cup of Nations]], Liberia drew their first game 1–1 with [[Mali national football team|Mali]] with [[George Weah]] scoring the goal and drew their second game 2–2 against [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]] behind goals from [[Prince Daye]] and [[Kelvin Sebwe]]. In their final group game, though, needing a win against [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] to advance, they lost 1–0.

=== FIFA World Cup ===

Liberia first entered the qualifying process for the FIFA World Cup in [[1966 FIFA World Cup qualification|1966]], however, they withdrew in protest against the fact that too few places had been reserved for Africa and Asia, along with all fourteen other African nations that had initially entered qualifying. 

Liberia next entered qualifying in [[1982 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1982]] against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] after receiving a bye in the first round. They lost 1–0 over the two legs and were eliminated. In [[1986 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1986]], Liberia was again eliminated without having scored a goal, falling 4–0 to [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]]. In [[1990 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1990]], Liberia won its first FIFA qualifying match defeating [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] to progress to the second round. Despite finishing second in their group, Liberia failed to advance to the final qualification round, ending two points behind group winners [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]].

In [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|1998]], Liberia beat [[Gambia national football team|Gambia]] in the first qualification round, but finished twelve points adrift of [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] in their group. In [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2002]], Liberia had their strongest qualifying campaign, however, a loss against [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]] in their penultimate group game gave [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] the opportunity to overtake them and secure the only qualification spot.

== Team image ==

=== Kit supplier ===
{| class="wikitable"
!Kit provider<ref>{{Cite web |title=Liberia Kit History |url=https://www.footballkitarchive.com/liberia-kits/ |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=Football Kit Archive |language=en-US |archive-date=November 1, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231101054855/https://www.footballkitarchive.com/liberia-kits/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
!Period
|-
|{{flagicon|DEN|}} [[Hummel International|Hummel]]
|1990–1993
|-
|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Diadora]]
|1994–1995
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|1996–1998
|-
|{{flagicon|Liberia}} Weah Sports
|1999–2001
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[Sportika]]
|2002
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|2003–2004
|-
|{{flagicon|DEN|}} [[Hummel International|Hummel]]
|2004–2006
|-
|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Kappa (brand)|Kappa]]
|2006–2008
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|2008
|-
|{{flagicon|Singapore}} [[Kubba Sportswear|Kubba]]
|2009
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|2010–2012
|-
|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Erreà]]
|2012
|-
|{{flagicon|Indonesia}} [[SPECS Sport|SPECS]]
|2012–2013
|-
|{{flagicon|ESP}} [[Joma]]
|2013–2014<ref>{{Cite web |last=Molaposc |date=2014-07-21 |title=Molapo Sports Centre: A good win for Lesotho, but there is still work to do |url=https://molaposportscentre.blogspot.com/2014/07/a-good-win-for-lesotho-but-still-work.html |access-date=2023-10-18 |website=Molapo Sports Centre |archive-date=November 1, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231101054856/https://molaposportscentre.blogspot.com/2014/07/a-good-win-for-lesotho-but-still-work.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
|2015–2020
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Puma SE|Puma]]
|2021–2024
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH}} [[Wanaw]]
|2024–present
|}

== Results and fixtures ==
{{main|Liberia national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

=== 2025 ===
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#TUN v LBR|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date = 4 September
|time = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TUN}}
|score = 3–0
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals1 =*[[Hazem Mastouri|Mastouri]] {{goal|5}}
*[[Ferjani Sassi|Sassi]] {{goal|66}}
*[[Elias Saad|Saad]] {{goal|90+4}} 
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Hammadi Agrebi Stadium]]
|location   = [[Tunis]], Tunisia
|attendance = 
|referee = 
|report = 
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#MWI v LBR|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date = 8 September
|time = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MWI}}
|score = 2–2
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals1 =*[[Gebandinho Mhango|Mhango]] {{goal|72}}
*[[Junior Yeanaye|Yeanaye]] {{goal|80|o.g.}} 
|goals2 =[[Ayouba Kosiah|Kosiah]] {{goal|3|61}} 
|stadium = [[Bingu National Stadium]]
|location   = [[Lilongwe]], Malawi 
|attendance = 
|referee = 
|report = 
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#LBR v NAM|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date = 9 October
|time = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score = 3–1
|team2 = {{fb|NAM}}
|goals1 = 
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Samuel Kanyon Doe Sports Complex]]
|location   = [[Paynesville, Liberia|Paynesville]], Liberia
|attendance = 
|referee = 
|report = 
|result = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group H#EQG v LBR|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date = 13 October
|time = {{UTZ|14:00|1}}
|team1 = {{fb-rt|EQG}}
|score = 1–1
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals1 = 
|goals2 = 
|stadium = [[Estadio de Malabo]]
|location   = [[Malabo]], Equatorial Guinea
|attendance = 
|referee = 
|report = 
|result = D
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 15 November
| time       = {{UTZ|17:30|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GUI}}
| score      = 2–0
| team2      = {{fb|LBR}}
| goals1     = *[[Sekou Tidiany Bangoura|Sekou Bangoura]] {{goal}}
*[[Yadaly Diaby]] {{goal|77}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
| location   = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| report     = https://guineenews.org/2025/11/15/maroc-la-guinee-simpose-2-0-face-au-liberia/
| result     = L
}}

=== 2026 ===
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|1}}
|date       = 27 March
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BEN}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Aiyegun Tosin|Tosin]] {{goal|85}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Jalal Jayed]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/benin-vs-liberia/4140065
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|1}}
|date       = 31 March
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score      = 2–2
|team2      = {{fb|LBY}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Oscar Dorley|Dorley]] {{goal|76}}
*[[Prince Balde|P. Balde]] {{goal|82}}
|goals2     =
*[[Ezoo El Maremi|El Maremi]] {{goal|44}}
*[[Al-Musrati]] {{goal|45+1}}
|stadium    = [[Larbi Zaouli Stadium]]
|location   = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Hamza El Fariq]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/liberia-vs-libya/4103855
|result     = D
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 6 June
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}} 
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|score      = 1–0
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-06/sierra-leone-vs-liberia/4315734
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SLE}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Abdoulie Jarjue Kabia|Jarjue Kabia]] {{goal|67}}
|team2      = {{fb|LBR}}
|goals2     = 
|stadium    = [[Bo Stadium]]
|location   = [[Freetown]], Sierra Leone
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 9 June
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}} 
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|score      = 3–1
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-09/liberia-vs-sierra-leone/4315736
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|goals1     = 
*Teclar {{goal|30}}
*[[Ayouba Kosiah|Kosiah]] {{goal|43||71}}
|team2      = {{fb|SLE}}
|goals2     = 
*[[Daniel Kanu (footballer)|D. Kanu]] {{goal|90+4}}
|stadium    = [[Samuel Kanyon Doe Sports Complex]]
|location   = [[Paynesville, Liberia|Paynesville]], Liberia
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CPV}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Liberia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CPV}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Cape Verde
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|RWA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Liberia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Rwanda
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MLI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|LBR}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Mali
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|LBR}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MLI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Liberia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!Position
!Name
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Head coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[Thomas Kojo]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;|Assistant coach
| style="text-align:left;|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[Kelvin Sebwe]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Assistant coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[Harold Gbotoe]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Goalkeeping coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[Chris Wesseh]]
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|Fitness coach
| style="text-align:left;"|{{flagicon|LBR}} [[Desmond Mulbah]]
|-
|}

===Coaching history===
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''
{{div col|colwidth=17em}}
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Josiah Johnson (footballer)|Josiah Johnson]] (1971–78)
* {{flagicon|FRG}} [[Bert Trautmann]] (1978–80)
* {{flagicon|BRA|1968}} [[Paulo Campos (football manager)|Paulo Campos]] (1986)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Walter Pelham]] (1986–1990)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Wilfred Lardner]] (1990–1998)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Kadalah Kromah]] (1999–2000)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Philippe Redon]] (2000–02)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Dominic George Vava]] (2002)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Kadalah Kromah]] (2002–04)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Joseph Sayon]] (2004–06)
* {{flagicon|EGY}} [[Shawky El Din]] (2006)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Frank Jericho Nagbe]] (2006–08)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Antoine Hey]] (2008–09)
* {{flagicon|HUN}} [[Bertalan Bicskei]] (2010–11)
* {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Roberto Landi]] (2011–12)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} ''[[Thomas Kojo]]'' (2012)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Kaetu Smith]] (2012)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Frank Jericho Nagbe]] (2013)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} ''[[Thomas Kojo]]'' (2013)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[James Debbah]] (2013–17)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Thomas Kojo]] (2018)
* {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Peter Butler (footballer, born 1966)|Peter Butler]] (2019–2022)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} ''[[Ansu Keita]]''  (2022)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} ''[[Thomas Kojo]]'' (2022–2023)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} [[Ansu Keita]]  (2023)
* {{flagicon|ROM}} [[Mario Marinica]]  (2024)
* {{flagicon|LBR}} ''[[Thomas Kojo]]'' (2025)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly]] matches against [[Benin national football team|Benin]] and [[Libya national football team|Libya]] on 27 and 31 March 2026; respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/permalink.php?story_fbid=pfbid0kieX5Y3suMFxShgMDwHy4nopiW1hPpEPD6QCcJwUhoTmo3uuZcaH8o4fDHyan9dSl&id=61565738660207|title=Here is the Lone Star's squad list for the two upcoming friendly matches|publisher=[[Liberia Football Association]]|access-date=22 May 2026|date=21 March 2026|via=[[Facebook]]}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 24 March 2025, after the match against [[Libya national football team|Libya]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Tommy Songo]]|age={{bda|1995|4|20|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[LISCR]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ashley Williams (Liberian footballer)|Ashley Williams]]|age={{bda|2000|10|30|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[FC Bea Mountain|Bea Mountain]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Junior Yeanaye|age={{bda|2004|12|19|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Wologizi FC|Wologizi]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Amos Wesseh]]|age={{bda|2000|12|19|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Rohda Raalte]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sampson Dweh]]|age={{bda|2001|10|10|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=1|club=[[Viktoria Plzeň]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Prince Balde]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|3|23|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=1|club=[[Trelleborgs FF]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Sebastien Teclay|age={{bda|2000|2|28|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[LISCR]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mark Pabai]]|age={{bda|2000|9|30|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[FC Koper|Koper]]|clubnat=SLO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Natus Swen|age={{bda|2004|11|18|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[FC Sheriff Tiraspol|Sheriff Tiraspol]]|clubnat=MDA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Nelson Laomie|age={{bda|2005|4|4|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Watanga FC|Watanga]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Emmanuel Fully]]|age={{bda|2006|3|20|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[SK Slavia Prague|Slavia Prague]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Tito Yormie|age={{bda|2002|1|24|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Neman Grodno|Neman Grodno]]|clubnat=BLR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Oscar Dorley]]|age={{bda|1998|7|19|df=y}}|caps=53|goals=4|club=[[SK Slavia Prague|Slavia Prague]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Divine Teah]]|age={{bda|2006|4|19|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=2|club=[[FK Pardubice|Pardubice]]|clubnat=CZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Solomon Tweh|age={{bda|2004|8|30|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Al Raed S.FC|Al-Raed]]|clubnat=KSA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Sheikh Sesay|age={{bda|2002|10|24|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[Gaborone United S.C.|Gaborone United]]|clubnat=BOT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Jegbay Konneh|age={{bda|2003|11|11|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Heaven Eleven FC|Heaven Eleven]]|clubnat=LBR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Edward Ledlum]]|age={{bda|1999|6|15|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=1|club=[[ASO Chlef]]|clubnat=ALG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Peter Wilson (footballer, born 1996)|Peter Wilson]]|age={{bda|1996|10|9|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=2|club=[[Oakland Roots SC]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=Sulahmana Bah|age={{birth date and age|2001|5|28|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=1|club=[[ZED FC|ZED]]|clubnat=EGY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jimmy Farkarlun]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|7|14|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=1|club=[[El Paso Locomotive FC|El Paso Locomotive]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=Emmanuel Flomo|age={{birth date and age|2007|10|31|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Al Hilal Club (Omdurman)|Al Hilal]]|clubnat=SDN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Emmanuel Gono]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|10|29|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[IK Oddevold]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=Prince Martor Jr|age={{birth date and age|2007|7|9|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Gençlerbirliği S.K.|Gençlerbirliği]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abdulai Koulibaly]]|age={{bda|1995|1|1|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Barrack Young Controllers FC|Barrack Young Controllers]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|MWI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Teddy Kollie|age={{bda|2005|11|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Watanga FC|Watanga]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|EQG}}, 9 September 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kemoh Kamara]]|age={{bda|1995|7|3|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club=[[FC Bea Mountain|Bea Mountain]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Philip Tarnue]]|age={{bda|2005|12|25|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Watanga FC|Watanga]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Josephus Mantor]]|age={{bda|2002|9|8|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Fassell|Fassell]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Meshach Greene]]|age={{bda|2003|4|23|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Paynesville FC|Paynesville]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jusu Dukuly]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|11|11|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Watanga FC|Watanga]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|EQG}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Jah Nyanforh|age={{birth date and age|2004|4|28|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Black Man Warrior|Black Man Warrior]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|MWI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mohammed Sangare]]|age={{bda|1998|12|28|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=4|club=[[Morecambe F.C.|Morecambe]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Armah Vaikainah]]|age={{bda|1995|6|17|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Marsaxlokk F.C.|Marsaxlokk]]|clubnat=MLT|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdulai Bility]]|age={{bda|1999|9|8|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Heaven Eleven FC|Heaven Eleven]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nohan Kenneh]]|age={{bda|2003|1|10|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Tranmere Rovers F.C.|Tranmere Rovers]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{Fb|MWI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Edward Alexander (footballer)|Edward Alexander]]|age={{bda|2005|9|29|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Mighty Barrolle|Mighty Barrolle]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|MWI}}, 8 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ayouba Kosiah]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2001|7|22}}|caps=12|goals=3|club=[[FK Radnik Surdulica|Radnik Surdulica]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Chauncy Freeman]]|age={{bda|2001|7|26|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Watanga FC|Watanga]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{Fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Daniel Toe]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|7|15|df=yes}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[LISCR FC|LISCR]]|clubnat=LBR|latest=v. {{fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Nicholas Andrews]]|age={{bda|df=y|1998|8|4}}|caps=6|goals=3|club=[[Fgura United F.C.|Fgura United]]|clubnat=MLT|latest=v. {{fb|GUI}}, 15 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[William Gibson (footballer, born 2007)|William Gibson]]|age={{bda|2007|11|22|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=2|club=[[Hammarby Talang FF]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=v. {{fb|EQG}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{updated|15 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Liberia |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/105/Liberia.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Liberia.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=30 |Rank
!width=150 |Name
!width=60 |Caps
!width=60 |Goals
!width=80 |Career
|-
| 1
| style="text-align:left"|[[Joe Nagbe]]{{efn|name=Nagbe|Nagbe retired from international duty in 2001 but later played two farewell matches in 2011.|group=}}
| 77
| 0
| 1986–2011
|-
| 2
| style="text-align:left"|[[George Weah]]{{efn|name=Weah|Weah retired from international duty in 2002 but later played a farewell match in 2018.|group=}}
| 75
| 18
| 1986–2018
|-
| 3
| style="text-align:left"|[[Kelvin Sebwe]]
| 62
| 10
| 1988–2008
|-
| 4
| style="text-align:left"|[[James Debbah]]{{efn|name=Debbah|Debbah retired from international duty in 2004 but later played a farewell match in 2018.|group=}}
| 58
| 13
| 1986–2018
|-
| 5
| style="text-align:left"|'''[[Oscar Dorley]]'''
| 54
| 3
| 2015–present
|-
| 6
| style="text-align:left"|[[George Gebro]]
| 48
| 1
| 1997–2012
|-
| 7
| style="text-align:left"|[[Anthony Laffor]]
| 46
| 5
| 2004–2018
|-
| 8
| style="text-align:left"|[[Teah Dennis Jr.]]
| 44
| 1
| 2011–2019
|-
| 9
| style="text-align:left"|[[Varmah Kpoto]]
| 40
| 1
| 1997–2008
|-
| rowspan=2|10
| style="text-align:left"|[[Fallah Johnson]]
| 37
| 0
| 1995–2004
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[Zizi Roberts]]
| 37
| 9
| 1995–2003
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Coppa UEFA 1992-93 - Napoli vs PSG - George Weah.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[George Weah]] is Liberia's top scorer with 18 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!width=30 |Rank
!width=120 |Name
!width=50 |Goals
!width=50 |Caps
!width=50 |Ratio
!width=80 |Career
|-
| 1
| style="text-align:left"|[[George Weah]]{{efn|name=Weah||group=}}
| 18
| 75
|{{#expr:18/75 round 2}}
| 1986–2018
|-
|rowspan=2|2
| style="text-align:left"|[[William Jebor]]
| 13
| 27
|{{#expr:13/27 round 2}}
| 2011–2023
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[James Debbah]]{{efn|name=Debbah||group=}}
| 13
| 58
|{{#expr:13/58 round 2}}
| 1986–2018
|-
| 4
| style="text-align:left"|[[Kelvin Sebwe]]
| 10
| 62
|{{#expr:10/62 round 2}}
| 1988–2008
|-
| 5
| style="text-align:left"|[[Zizi Roberts]]
| 9
| 37
|{{#expr:9/37 round 2}}
| 1995–2003
|-
| 6
| style="text-align:left"|[[Oliver Makor]]
| 8
| 32
|{{#expr:8/32 round 2}}
| 1995–2008
|-
| rowspan=2|7
| style="text-align:left"|[[Prince Daye]]
| 7
| 25
|{{#expr:7/25 round 2}}
| 1996–2004
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[Jonathan Sogbie]]
| 7
| 27
|{{#expr:7/27 round 2}}
| 1988–1998
|-
| 9
| style="text-align:left"|[[Zah Rahan Krangar|Zah Rahan]]
| 6
| 35
|{{#expr:6/35 round 2}}
| 2006–2017
|-
| rowspan=5|10
| style="text-align:left"|[[Francis Doe]]
| 5
| 22
|{{#expr:5/22 round 2}}
| 2004–2016
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[Isaac Tondo]]
| 5
| 22
|{{#expr:5/22 round 2}}
| 2000–2005
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[Dioh Williams]]
| 5
| 24
|{{#expr:5/24 round 2}}
| 2004–2016
|-
| style="text-align:left"|'''[[Marcus Macauley]]'''
| 5
| 28
|{{#expr:5/28 round 2}}
| 2011–present
|-
| style="text-align:left"|[[Anthony Laffor]]
| 5
| 46
|{{#expr:5/46 round 2}}
| 2004–2018
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=38|
! colspan="6" |[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]] to {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|8
|3
|3
|2
|4
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualification''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualification''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|8
|2
|1
|5
|7
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|10
|6
|1
|3
|11
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|12
|2
|1
|9
|6
|29
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|0
|3
|3
|4
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|6
|1
|1
|4
|3
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|4
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||3||0||5||8||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10||4||3||3||13||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!—
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!–
!76
!22
!15
!39
!60
!105
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{See also|Liberia at the Africa Cup of Nations}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 2
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]]||rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 African Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 African Cup of Nations|1963]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 African Cup of Nations|1965]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 African Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 African Cup of Nations|1970]]
|rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Cup of Nations|1974]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|Group stage
|13th
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 African Cup of Nations|2002]]
|Group stage
|9th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|3
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 African Cup of Nations|2004]]
|rowspan=12 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
|rowspan=1 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Group stage'''
!'''2/35'''
!'''5'''
!'''1'''
!'''2'''
!'''2'''
!'''5'''
!'''7'''
|}

==Honors==
===Regional===
* '''[[WAFU Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): 2011
* '''[[West African Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): 1987
* '''[[CEDEAO Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): 1987

==Notes==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commonscat}}
* [http://Lfa-lr.com/ Official Website]
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/lbr/ Liberia] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/LBR Liberia] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20060220014841/http://www.liberiansoccer.com/home.htm Liberian-Soccer.com] fan site
* [https://liberiapublicradio.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/LONE-STAR-TEAM.jpg Liberia national football team]

{{Liberia national football team}}
{{Navboxes top
|bg = #BF0A30
|fg = white
|bordercolor = #002868
|list1=}}
{{Liberia national football team managers}}
{{Navboxes
|title = Liberia squads
|bg = #BF0A30
|fg = white
|bordercolor = #002868
|list1=
{{Liberia squad 1996 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Liberia squad 2002 Africa Cup of Nations}}
}}
{{Football in Liberia}}
{{WAFU football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Liberia}}
{{Navboxes bottom}}
{{#invoke:Portal bar|main|Association football|Liberia}}

[[Category:Liberia national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
